parts.content:
Write 1 dissimilar SBA question(s) on: 'This patient has severe ventilatory failure with marked hypercapnia, acidaemia, hypoxaemia and reduced consciousness.'.

Each question should test application of knowledge, not simple recall — a candidate should need to reason from principles rather than just retrieve a fact. Use the retrieve tool to find relevant reference material before writing. Return the result as a QuestionSet.

parts.args: {"query": 'This patient has severe ventilatory failure with marked hypercapnia, acidaemia, hypoxaemia and reduced consciousness.'}

{"topic":"Primary Frca Sample Sbas","exam":"primary","curriculum_node_code":null,"model":"openai:gpt-5.5","generated_at":"2026-05-02T01:16:45.156172","questions":[{"stem":"A 67-year-old man has been admitted to intensive care with an infective exacerbation of COPD. He is receiving antibiotics, steroids and synchronised non-invasive ventilation with FiO2 0.6, inspiratory pressure 12 cmH2O and expiratory pressure 5 cmH2O. He becomes unrousable with respiratory rate 12 min-1. Arterial blood gas shows pH 7.14, PaCO2 11.2 kPa, PaO2 7.4 kPa, HCO3- 34 mmol L-1 and base excess 7.4.","lead":"What is the most appropriate next step in management?","options":[{"text":"Doxapram intravenously","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Doxapram is a respiratory stimulant but is rarely appropriate when a patient is unrousable with severe acidaemia and an unprotected airway."},{"text":"Increase FiO2 to 0.8","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Increasing FiO2 may improve oxygenation but will not address ventilatory failure and may worsen hypercapnia in COPD."},{"text":"Increase inspiratory pressure","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Increasing pressure support may improve ventilation, but this patient is unconscious and cannot protect his airway, so NIV is no longer appropriate."},{"text":"Invasive ventilation","is_correct":true,"explanation":"The patient has severe acute-on-chronic hypercapnic respiratory failure with depressed consciousness and an unprotected airway, requiring tracheal intubation and invasive ventilation."},{"text":"Reduce FiO2 to 0.4","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Although excessive oxygen can worsen hypercapnia in COPD, this patient is dangerously hypoxaemic and reducing FiO2 would be unsafe."}],"explanation":"This patient has severe ventilatory failure with marked hypercapnia, acidaemia, hypoxaemia and reduced consciousness. NIV is contraindicated or failing when the patient cannot protect the airway, so intubation and invasive ventilation are required.","curriculum_node_codes":["1_GA_K_2","1_RT_B_7","1_GA_K_4","1_ICM_C_1","1_GA_K"],"curriculum_node_scores":[0.6542108474077111,0.6461963490378206,0.6158445566584287,0.5847704428726317,0.5711400136859766]}]}
